metadata.note: Control of strategic port infrastructure in the Americas -- concession disputes, court-ordered operator changes, and the coercive retaliation over them (Panama/CK Hutchison, Peru/Chancay). Multilateral archetype, primary_target=null. NAME-GATED (A2-like): every alias is a named canal/port/operator; generic domain nouns are deliberately EXCLUDED because aliases are OR'd and the centroid gate spans AMERICAS-USA + ASIA-CHINA. Pre-audit against 180d corpus: 'concession' 64 in-gate hits / 1 on-topic, 'terminal' 53/1, 'arbitration' 16/7, 'Cosco' 10/2 (rest Hormuz shipping), 'Maersk' 18 in-gate with 7 Hormuz/tariff leaks and ZERO unique on-topic contribution (every on-topic Maersk title also carries Panama or Hutchison) -- all dropped. Bare 'Panama' is NOT A2-safe: flag-of-convenience noise (Black Sea drone strike, IRGC missile near Umm Qasr, Odesa, cargo sinking off Western Sahara). Compounds only. Non-Latin lists are near-zero in the current corpus (Chinese state media publish this story in English, same as the SCS build) but are kept in native orthography for future ingest.

bundle.term: The canal port terminals (the contested concession)
Maritime coercion over the dispute (flag-state retaliation)

es: control por el Estado rector del puerto
